extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won the 238km stage sprint in Zabrze after VAN POPPEL Danny led him out from 800m but faded in final 50m.
tactical_patterns: VAN POPPEL Danny forced to lead from 800m out burned matches before finish, enabling EWAN to save energy.
SAGAN Peter threw for bonus seconds not victory, prioritizing GC gap protection over stage win.
Extreme heat (40°C) neutralized pure power sprinters, favoring lighter riders like EWAN in finale.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: First win since Giro stage, demonstrated mental resilience after early-week losses when needed confidence boost
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: negative: Tactical error leading too early from 800m exposed lack of finisher support in Lotto squad
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Shoulder contact mid-sprint then apologized, calculated bonus seconds priority shows GC focus over stage hunting
rider_signals.BATTAGLIN Enrico: positive: Sixth place exceptional for rider without 2017 contract at season start, gaining 2018 leverage
rider_signals.PORSEV Alexander: negative: Fourteenth place disappointing for Gazprom sprinter expectations on flat 238km stage
rider_signals.HAIG Jack: positive: Protected EWAN all day despite Orica down to six riders, key domestique work enabled victory
